channel.title: InsurTech Intelligence
channel.description: Noticias globales de insurtech, actualizadas cada 6 horas.

item.title: Ford Recalling 548,000 US Vehicles Over Defective Center Console
item.description: Ford is recalling 548,463 vehicles in the United States over an issue with the center console, the U.S. Related: Stellantis Tells Owners of 1.3M Jeeps to Park Outside Over Fire Concerns The recall, which affects certain 2018-2024 Ford Expedition vehicles, may have been caused by the center console chrome trim that was manufactured by a supplier using parameters that did not meet Ford specifications, the NHTSA said. Related: Ford Recalling 420,000 US Vehicles Over Seat-Belt Issue (Reporting by Christy in Bengaluru; Editing by Sherry Jacob-Phillips) Get the latest insurance news sent straight to your inbox.

item.title: Man Sentenced Over False FEMA Claims for Lahaina, California Wildfires
item.description: Daylyn Harris, 35, of Honolulu, was sentenced to 24 months in prison, followed by five years of supervised release, for conspiring to commit wire fraud in connection with false claims submitted to FEMA seeking disaster relief related to the wildfires as well as a separate obstruction of justice charge. A Hawaii man was sentenced for filing claims with the Federal Emergency Management Agency seeking disaster relief related to the Lahaina and Pacific Palisades wildfires. Harris falsely claimed he resided in Lahaina during the Lahaina wildfires, and that he lost housing, income, suffered medical bills, and lost property as a result.

item.title: US Workers’ Health Insurance Costs Set to Rise, Survey Finds
item.description: companies plan to charge more for employee health plans next year, as soaring drug prices drive up insurance costs. Two thirds of large companies expect to raise monthly premiums for employee health coverage through paycheck deductions in 2027, according to a survey of businesses with at least 500 employees by benefits consultancy Mercer. Health insurers are raising costs for employers, too, with the cost of group plans set to increase by more than 6% for the fourth year in a row, said Beth Umland, Mercer’s director of research.

item.title: Berkley Re’s Glen Riddell named chair of Singapore Reinsurers’ Association
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: Munich Re Specialty promotes Philip Steinbach to head of surety underwriting for Australia
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: Liberty, Marsh, Lockton, Guy Carpenter, Markel: 15 APAC insurance people moves of the week
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: NZ Natural Hazards Commission renews record reinsurance tower, 20% increase to $12.3bn
item.description: With reinsurance market conditions particularly conducive for buyers to increase the amount of limit they renew, it seems the New Zealand Natural Hazards Commission has capitalised on that to grow its reinsurance tower by an even bigger margin this year. New Zealand’s Natural Hazards Commission Toka Tū Ake (NHC), formerly known as New Zealand’s Earthquake Commission (EQC), took advantage of attractive reinsurance buying conditions at its 2026 renewal to lift the top of its reinsurance tower by 20% to $12.3 billion. That’s around $2.1 billion of additional reinsurance limit secured at the renewals, which the New Zealand Natural Hazards Commission has said is a record size and was secured with efficient pricing.

item.title: Mercury raises Cal fire reinsurance target to $125m-$175m with second Luca Re cat bond
item.description: Mercury General Corporation (Mercury Insurance), the California headquartered insurer, is now aiming to upsize its second 144A catastrophe bond sponsorship, with the target for fire reinsurance limit from the Luca Re Ltd. Mercury Insurance sponsored its debut 144A catastrophe bond in 2025, when it secured $150 million of California fire reinsurance protection from the capital markets from the first Luca Re 2025-1 deal. The Series 2026-1 cat bond notes that Luca Re is offering will provide Mercury with a roughly three-year source of collateralized reinsurance protection against wildfire and fire-following earthquake losses in the state of California, on an indemnity trigger and per-occurrence basis.

item.title: Willis Re continues leadership expansion with Ogilvie and Dart hires in London & Bermuda
item.description: In their new roles, Ogilvie, based in London, and Dart, based in Bermuda, will hold responsibility for leading their respective teams, managing portfolio performance, and contributing to the expansion of Willis Re’s reinsurance platform. Willis Re, the reinsurance advisory and broking joint venture between WTW and Bain Capital, has announced that it has appointed Jonathan Ogilvie as Global Head of Non-Marine Specialties, and Chris Dart as Head of Treaty Reinsurance, Bermuda, effective immediately. Before he joined Willis Re, Ogilvie served as Head of Property Retrocession in Bermuda at broker Guy Carpenter and previously held management positions within the company’s London operations, working across property, specialty and casualty lines.

item.title: Willis Re expands leadership team with two senior appointments in London and Bermuda
item.description: Willis Re said Ogilvie, based in London, and Dart, based in Bermuda, will lead their respective teams, oversee portfolio performance and support the continued expansion of its reinsurance platform. “Their appointments represent an important step in our continued development, enhancing our capabilities, broadening our market reach and positioning us to support clients globally with increasingly sophisticated solutions.” Ogilvie has more than 20 years of experience across the insurance, reinsurance and retrocession markets in both London and Bermuda. Before joining Willis Re, he served as Head of Property Retrocession in Bermuda at Guy Carpenter and previously held management positions within the firm’s London operations, working across property, specialty and casualty lines.

item.title: Descartes and Nextpower launch parametric insurance solution for extreme wind conditions
item.description: Descartes Underwriting, a specialist in corporate parametric re/insurance solutions for climate and emerging risks, and Nextpower™, a provider of solar and power technology solutions for utility-scale power plants, have partnered to launch an integrated parametric insurance solution for extreme wind conditions. This partnership reflects our commitment to supporting the resilience of critical renewable infrastructure in the global energy transition economy.” Descartes’ climate scientists and natural risk modellers leverage real-time wind speed data from Nextpower’s on-site meteorological stations to set parametric insurance triggers. While Nextpower’s tracking systems provide word-class physical resilience against severe wind, the Descartes policy has been designed to complement as a financial backstop for extreme weather events that exceed normal operating conditions.

item.title: QBE adds Christopher Harris as Independent Non‑Executive Director
item.description: International insurer and reinsurer QBE has appointed Christopher Harris as an Independent Non‑Executive Director to the QBE Group Board, effective July 6th, 2026, subject to regulatory approvals. Yasmin Allen AM, Chair of the Board of Directors, QBE, commented on the appointment, “Christopher brings deep industry expertise, particularly in capital allocation, risk management and underwriting, along with a strong track record as both an executive and director. Currently, Harris serves as the Chair of QBE Capital.

item.title: People Moves: CRC Specialty Names Levy National ExecPro Leader; GEICO Names Foy CFO; Geller to Lead Wholesale Financial Lines at AXIS
item.description: CRC Specialty Names Levy National ExecPro Leader CRC Group appointed Florence Levy as national ExecPro leader. Geller to Lead Wholesale Financial Lines at AXIS AXIS Capital Holdings Limited , headquartered in Pembroke, Bermuda, promoted Christina Geller to head of wholesale financial lines in North America. Geller will develop and deliver the strategy for long-term profitable growth across AXIS’ portfolio of North American wholesale commercial and financial institutions business.

item.title: Trump Insists Iran Deal Is Close After Scrapping New Strikes
item.description: President Donald Trump pulled back threatened military strikes against Iran, a stark reversal that came just hours after he vowed to hit the Islamic Republic “VERY HARD” and threatened to seize its oil infrastructure. But then in the afternoon, Trump announced on social media the attacks were off, claiming again that a deal was close — without any confirmation from Iran. US forces had already pounded Iran with airstrikes for two straight days when Trump on Thursday morning said they would do so for a third.

item.title: UK Venture Plans Europe’s Largest Plant for Capturing CO2 From Air
item.description: UnionDAC’s plant, if it reaches its target, would overtake Climeworks AG’s Mammoth project in Iceland as Europe’s largest direct air capture facility. Low-carbon project developer Progressive Energy , together with carbon-capture specialists Airhive and Mission Zero Technologies , has formed a new company to build a plant in northeast England capable of capturing carbon dioxide directly from the atmosphere. Three UK clean-technology companies are teaming up to create what may be Europe’s biggest direct air capture facility.

item.title: New Zealand secures record NZ$12.3bn reinsurance programme to strengthen disaster resilience
item.description: The post New Zealand secures record NZ$12.3bn reinsurance programme to strengthen disaster resilience appeared first on Insurtech Insights . New Zealand’s Natural Hazards Commission Toka Tū Ake (NHC) has secured a record NZ$12.3 billion in reinsurance protection for 2026, marking a 20% increase from the NZ$10.3 billion programme arranged last year and reinforcing the country’s financial resilience against major natural hazard events. With catastrophe losses continuing to rise globally, New Zealand’s ability to secure record levels of reinsurance at improved cost efficiency may also position the country as a benchmark for public-private disaster financing and resilience planning.

item.title: Zurich Australia partners with Crop Risk Underwriting
item.description: Zurich Australia has partnered with Crop Risk Underwriting (CRU), a specialist crop insurance underwriting agency and part of the 360 Group of Companies, to provide crop insurance in Australia from June 1, 2026. By combining Zurich’s global risk expertise with CRU’s specialist underwriting capability, the partnership aims to deliver more data-driven, transparent and responsive insurance solutions to the Australian agricultural sector.” – Peter Book, General Manager, CRU. Through the new agreement, Zurich will provide capacity for CRU’s crop insurance products covering broadacre and cotton, protecting customers from hail and fire risks.

item.title: Middle East conflict: world ‘in the foothills’ of prolonged war, insurers told
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: Specialist broker HWF Partners explores Asia entry as transaction liability talent war intensifies

item.title: Axa XL Re’s Phil Xue returns to Guy Carpenter as China, Hong Kong managing director
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: El Niño Conditions Are Present, US Forecaster Says
item.description: El Niño conditions are present and expected to strengthen into the Northern Hemisphere winter of 2026–27, the U.S. government forecaster added that El Niño conditions developed over the past month. Related: CSU Adjusts Atlantic Hurricane Season Forecast Due to Emerging El Niño The U.S.

item.title: Remote Work Makes it Hard for Young Workers to Find Jobs: Study
item.description: The rise of remote work since the pandemic has made businesses more reluctant to hire young, inexperienced workers and is the key driver of higher unemployment rates for recent college graduates, a study released Monday has found. “Remote work has weakened incentives to hire young workers by impeding on-the-job training,” the study said. The authors of the study calculate that remote work is responsible for nearly two-thirds of the rise in the unemployment rate for young college graduates since the pandemic.

item.title: It’s official: NOAA declares start of El Niño
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: Base10 Partners Closes 2 Funds Totaling $850M To Invest In Real Economy Automation
item.description: San Francisco-based Base10 Partners has raised two funds totaling $850 million: a seed and Series A fund 4, and a Series B fund 2 to invest in automation for the real economy. The firm has a strong focus on logistics, payroll, construction and other real economy sectors. Ajao asks: Can AI understand manufacturing processes the way LLMs understand text, whether it’s perfumes, pharmaceuticals, chips or concrete, for real economy applications?

item.title: Before You Cheer The IPO Window, Watch Where The Money Goes
item.description: After four years of a venture liquidity drought, the read across the industry is simple: the IPO window is finally open again. If you run an early-stage company, the window reopening for SpaceX does very little for you directly. Don’t build for an IPO window that was only ever open to a handful of companies.

item.title: Lauren Pratscher joins Aon as Middle Market Chief Broking Officer
item.description: Aon, a global re/insurance broker and risk advisor, has announced the appointment of Lauren Pratscher as Chief Broking Officer for the company’s Middle Market P&C broking operation. Pratscher brings over two decades of industry experience to her new position, having most recently served as Head of Casualty Placement at Newfront – acquired by WTW in 2026. Pratscher also served as a North Central Casualty Growth Leader, a position she held following her role as Senior Casualty Advisory Specialist.

item.title: US commercial insurance rates up 2.5% in Q1’26 amid softening trends: WTW
item.description: Year-on-year, US commercial insurance rates fell from the aggregate price increase of 5.3% seen in Q1’25, reflecting that pricing trends have continued to soften across most commercial lines, including small commercial, mid-market commercial, and large account commercial accounts. According to global insurance and reinsurance broker WTW’s latest Commercial Lines Insurance Pricing Survey (CLIPS), commercial insurance rates across the US increased 2.5% in the first quarter of 2026, the third consecutive quarter of moderating rate rises. “While Commercial Auto and Excess/Umbrella Liability continue to experience the largest increases, the pace of those increases has eased, with pricing trends across much of the market remaining stable.” WTW’s CLIPS is a survey that measures changes in commercial insurance pricing by comparing premiums for policies underwritten during the quarter with those for the same coverage lines in the prior year.

item.title: Tesla’s Robotaxi Falls Short With Long Waits and Stalled Rides
item.description: “We’ll probably have autonomous ride-hailing in about half the population of the US by the end of the year.” Almost a year later, Tesla has just 59 vehicles in its entire robotaxi fleet as of Tuesday, limited to three Texas cities. “For well over a decade, Musk has overpromised… Add the robotaxi rollout to the list.” Tesla shares fell 1% at 9:30 a.m. Tesla was planning to grow further in the city and quickly spread to California, Nevada, Arizona and Florida.

item.title: Union Bay Acquisition Acquires Michigan-Based Arlington Agency
item.description: Union Bay Acquisition, an aggregator of insurance agencies, has acquired the assets of Arlington Agency, Inc., based in Livonia, Michigan. Based in Lancaster, Pennsylvania, Union Bay Acquisition owns property-casualty insurance agent Union Bay Risk Advisors LLC, and acquires small insurance agencies in the Northeast and Midwest. This is Union Bay’s eighteenth acquisition, and fifth in the state of Michigan.

item.title: Nebraska Settles With Company Regarding Overpriced COVID Tests
item.description: Nebraska Attorney General Hilgers announced a $4.87 million multistate settlement with GS Labs that resolves claims that the testing company overcharged patients, unlawfully charged administrative fees, and failed to deliver timely COVID-19 test results. GS Labs significantly overcharged Nebraskans for COVID tests. Under the terms of today’s settlement, GS Labs will pay $3,628,718.34 in restitution to patients, including $1,843,375.99 for cash-paying patients that were overcharged for tests, $1,749,568.35 for patients that were charged administrative fees, and $33,692 for cash-paying patients that did not receive test results within three days.

item.title: $7.7bn Amwins and Dragoneer bid for Steadfast signals major shake-up in insurance broking
item.description: The post $7.7bn Amwins and Dragoneer bid for Steadfast signals major shake-up in insurance broking appeared first on Insurtech Insights . Australian insurance broker Steadfast Group has received a A$7.7bn acquisition proposal from a consortium comprising Amwins Group and Dragoneer Investment Group, in a move that could reshape Australia’s insurance distribution market. Under the proposed structure, Dragoneer would acquire ownership of Steadfast’s retail brokerage business, while Amwins would take control of the group’s underwriting agency operations.

item.title: Coinbase launches Coinbase for Agents
item.description: Crypto exchange Coinbase is introducing Coinbase for Agents, a new feature that allows users to connect their favorite AI agents directly to their Coinbase accounts to trade, pay, and operate within limits they control. As that ecosystem grows, Coinbase for Agents is positioned to be your trading and spending account at the center of it.” – Coinbase. Agents will be able to help users rebalance their portfolio, monitor cash position and execute preset moves, and leverage premium data – agents can pay for data and services for data-informed trades.

item.title: Canopius appoints Steve Parry as Group Chief Claims Officer
item.description: Canopius has appointed Steve Parry as Group Chief Claims Officer, effective August 2026. Parry joins from Hiscox, where he served as Group Claims Director, and previously held senior claims leadership roles at AIG and ACE/Chubb. Parry will join the Canopius Group Leadership Team and report to Group CEO Neil Robertson.

item.title: CAC expands contingent risk practice into Europe with London launch
item.description: CAC Group , part of The Baldwin Group, has expanded its Contingent Risk practice into Europe with the launch of a London office led by Nick Moore. Moore brings more than 15 years of experience spanning litigation, arbitration, litigation finance, and contingent risk management. Most recently, he led litigation and contingent risk initiatives across the UK and EMEA at a global insurance broker.

item.title: U.S. Forecaster Says El Niño Conditions Present
item.description: El Niño conditions are present and expected to strengthen into the Northern Hemisphere winter of 2026–27, the U.S. government forecaster added that El Niño conditions developed over the past month. El Niño is a phenomenon that occurs naturally when weakening trade winds cause warmer ocean waters to build up in the central and eastern equatorial Pacific.

item.title: Surge in Catalytic Converters Thefts Highlights Top 10 Vehicles Targeted: CARFAX
item.description: “Experts also tell us that while thieves can make anywhere from $25-$300 for a standard catalytic converter, converters from hybrid vehicles can sell for up to $1,400 because they have even more of those precious metals inside.” Rising precious metal prices are one of the main factors behind the increase in thefts. A rise in value has caused an increase in catalytic converter thefts across the U.S., according to new data from vehicle history provider CARFAX. Here are the top vehicles most frequently targeted nationwide: “There is a wide range of vehicles impacted, and most of these are pickup trucks and SUVs, which tend to sit higher off the ground, making it easier for thieves to get in and out,” said Patrick Olsen , editor-in-chief at CARFAX.

item.title: Amplifi Capital (U.K.) Limited enters administration
item.description: On 9 June 2026, Amplifi Capital (U.K.) Limited (Amplifi) entered administration. Amplifi trades under the names Reevo Money and My Community Finance. All existing loan agreements remain in place and will not change because of the administration.

item.title: Court orders appointment of special administrators for Euro Exchange Securities UK Limited
item.description: The High Court today confirmed the appointment of special administrators for Euro Exchange Securities UK Limited (EES). Duncan Perring and James Bennett of Teneo Financial Advisory Limited have been appointed as joint special administrators, under the Payment and Electronic Money Institution Insolvency Regulations 2021. EES agreed it is not in the company’s interests to seek to return to normal trading and will work with the appointed special administrators to ensure client money is returned as quickly as possible.

item.title: The $100M+ Round Is Now Just Your Typical Late-Stage Financing
item.description: The rise of the $100 million-plus round hasn’t been chronologically linear, as charted below: Initially, the category gained traction in the late 2010s, as companies such as Uber , Rivian and WeWork scaled up late-stage financing in advance of plans for public offerings. Per Crunchbase data, the typical financing at this stage has roughly doubled since 2020, from just over $50 million to around $100 million. late-stage round this year was exactly $100 million.

item.title: Willis Re hires Guy Carpenter’s John Fletcher as CEO of Bermuda
item.description: Willis Re, the reinsurance advisory and broking joint venture between WTW and Bain Capital, has appointed reinsurance and retrocession veteran John Fletcher from Guy Carpenter as Chief Executive Officer of its Bermuda platform, effective immediately. Most recently, Fletcher served as CEO of Bermuda at broker Guy Carpenter, having led the company’s Bermuda operations since 2018. As CEO of Willis Re Bermuda, Fletcher will hold responsibility for driving business growth and the continued development of the team.

item.title: Descartes partners with Nextpower to launch parametric wind coverage for solar plants
item.description: Descartes Underwriting, a specialist underwriter of parametric risk transfer opportunities, has partnered with Nextpower to launch an integrated parametric insurance solution that uses site-level wind data from Nextpower meteorological stations to protect solar power plant operators against the economic impact of straight-line wind (SLW) events. The companies also emphasised that through this integrated parametric solution, solar power plant owners and operators with Nextpower systems will gain a significant financial safeguard against damaging wind events. The launch of this solution comes as straight-line wind (SLW) remains one of the most destructive and underinsured perils facing the global solar industry.

item.title: LRS opens door to explore further risks, optimistic for Brazil’s ILS regime: Avla’s Astrachan
item.description: After working with Gálapagos Capital SSPE on a recent R$126 million offering of Insurance Risk Letters (LRS), Avla Brazil CEO Felippe Astrachan has said that LRS opens a door that allows the company to explore different types of risks that are not traditionally covered by the insurance and reinsurance market, while also remaining optimistic about the future of the expanding Brazil ILS regime. Artemis spoke to Astrachan to find out what motivated Avla to explore the insurance-linked securities (ILS) market, and specifically the LRS instrument as a way to transfer risk. “LRS open a door that allows us to explore types of risks that are not traditionally covered by the insurance and reinsurance market.

item.title: Lockton’s revenue rises 12% as reinsurance business continues strong trajectory
item.description: Lockton, an independent insurance broker, has reported a 12% year-over-year increase in global revenue to approximately $4.5 billion for fiscal year 2026, with continued strong growth at Lockton Re, the firm’s global reinsurance business. Lockton highlighted that its US business delivered another strong year, with revenue of just under $3 billion, up 11% year-over-year, driven by strong client retention and continued growth across key areas of the business. The firm noted that the revenue increase included 11% organic growth, marking Lockton’s sixth consecutive year of double-digit organic growth and underscoring the strength of its client relationships, diversified business model, and long-term investment strategy.

item.title: AXIS appoints new senior underwriter in US healthcare reinsurance
item.description: AXIS, a global specialty insurer and reinsurer, has appointed Brian Scott as Senior Underwriter within its US Healthcare Reinsurance team. Scott joins AXIS with close to twenty years of experience across the healthcare sector, having previously held roles at RGA Reinsurance, Voya Financial, Point6 Healthcare and Lockton Companies. AXIS states that its approach to US Healthcare Reinsurance is focused on understanding what its partners are seeking to protect and the key factors contributing to portfolio volatility.

item.title: DXC to leverage Anthropic’s Claude for mission-critical enterprise systems
item.description: These specialists will focus on integrating Claude models into production inside the mission-critical technology infrastructure systems DXC operates for major global entities, including re/insurers, banks, airlines, manufacturers, and government agencies. For the insurance sector, DXC aims to leverage Claude to deploy agentic solutions and transform core systems aligned to each firm’s unique context, operating model, and strategic intent. DXC Technology, a global technology firm with a specialism in insurance and reinsurance, has entered into a multi-year global partnership with Anthropic, an artificial intelligence (AI) safety company and creator of Claude.

item.title: WTW: US Commercial Rates Continue Moderation With 2.5% Increase in Q1
item.description: commercial insurance rates increased 2.5% in the first quarter 2026, according to the latest WTW Commercial Lines Insurance Pricing Survey (CLIPS). “While Commercial Auto and Excess/Umbrella Liability continue to experience the largest increases, the pace of those increases has eased, with pricing trends across much of the market remaining stable.” According to prior results, Q1 was the third consecutive quarter of moderating rate increases. Rates increased 2.9% in Q4 2025 and nearly 3.3% for Q3 2025.

item.title: Ford to Recall More Than 548,000 US Vehicles Over Defective Center Console
item.description: Ford is recalling 548,463 vehicles in the United States over an issue with the center console, the U.S. The recall, which affects certain 2018-2024 Ford Expedition vehicles, may have been caused by the center console chrome trim that was manufactured by a supplier using parameters that did not meet Ford specifications, the NHTSA said. The issue relates to the console’s chrome plating, which may bubble and peel over time, potentially resulting in sharp edges.

item.title: Lockton Reports Organic Revenue Growth of 11% for Fiscal Year 2026
item.description: Lockton, the privately held, independent insurance brokerage, reported strong financial results for fiscal year 2026, with global revenues increasing 12% year over year to approximately $4.5 billion, with organic growth of 11%. This performance marks Lockton’s sixth consecutive year of double‑digit organic growth, Lockton said. “Fiscal year 2026 was another exceptional year for Lockton,” said Ron Lockton, chairman and chief executive officer, in a statement.

item.title: Duck Creek expands global platform strategy with London Market push and Lloyd’s certification plans
item.description: The post Duck Creek expands global platform strategy with London Market push and Lloyd’s certification plans appeared first on Insurtech Insights . Duck Creek Technologies has unveiled an expanded global platform strategy and new investments aimed at supporting insurers operating in the London Market, including plans to pursue Lloyd’s market certification. The move includes plans to pursue Lloyd’s market certification, a step aimed at strengthening Duck Creek’s position among insurers and managing agents operating within the global specialty marketplace.

item.title: $50m OpenAI-backed Poetic emerges from stealth to automate insurance underwriting and compliance
item.description: The post $50m OpenAI-backed Poetic emerges from stealth to automate insurance underwriting and compliance appeared first on Insurtech Insights . AI startup Poetic has emerged from stealth with $50m in funding and a $500m valuation, positioning itself as a new player in insurance underwriting, compliance, and fraud automation. Founded by former Google and Waymo machine-learning engineer Markie Wagner, Poetic is targeting some of finance’s most complex operational processes, including insurance underwriting, compliance investigations, and fraud detection.
